title: 10 best herbal tinctures for cystitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for cystitis are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, used to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections (UTIs). These tinctures help by reducing inflammation, combating bacteria, and soothing the bladder. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with cystitis include Stinging Nettle, Uva Ursi, and Marshmallow Root. By using these tinctures, individuals can reduce frequency and urgency of urination, alleviate burning sensations while urinating, and experience relief from pain and discomfort. This improves lives by allowing people to manage symptoms naturally, reducing the need for antibiotics and antihistamines.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry tinctures helps with cystitis because of its natural antibacterial properties that target the bacterial infection causing the condition. The tannins present in bearberry extract have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. Additionally, bearberry's astringent properties help to reduce urination frequency and soothe the bladder lining, providing relief from burning sensations during urination. By addressing the root causes of cystitis, bearberry tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing symptoms and promoting urinary tract health.
remedy_properties: Uva Ursi's ability to reduce inflammation: Bearberry tincture contains compounds like arbutin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.

Its antimicrobial effects: The tannins present in bearberry tincture have antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying infection that causes cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery.

Bearberry's ability to acidify urine: Bearberry tincture contains a compound called arbutoside, which can help increase the acidity of urine, creating an environment that is less conducive to bacterial growth, thereby helping to treat and prevent cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Uvaursenone: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate the symptoms of cystitis by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation in the urinary tract.

Arctiopicrin: This triterpenoid saponin has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, thus providing relief from cystitis symptoms.

Arctostaphyloside: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
Barks: Utilized for their antimicrobial and astringent properties to help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
Roots: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh or dried uva ursi berries and chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped berries with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and label it clearly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with cystitis because of its soothing properties, which provide relief from inflammation and discomfort associated with this condition. The saponin-rich extract of marshmallow root coats the urinary tract lining, reducing irritation and pain caused by bacterial infections or allergic reactions. Additionally, herbal marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate bladder spasms and burning sensations often experienced during cystitis episodes. As a result, it can provide natural and effective relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in marshmallow tincture have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial infections that may contribute to cystitis and promote healing of the bladder lining.

Mucilaginous and protective: Marshmallow root's mucilage coats and protects the urinary tract lining, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation caused by infection or other factors, and promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Gallic acid: As a phenolic compound, gallic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the pain and inflammation caused by cystitis.

Mucilages: These thick, protective substances help form a protective barrier on the bladder and urinary tract mucosa, reducing irritation and inflammation, and providing relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of mucilage, a key component in treating cystitis due to its soothing and protective properties.
Leaves: Leaves are rich in mucilage and other compounds that help alleviate inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Barks: The bark of Althaea officinalis is also used for its mucilaginous properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part althaea officinalis root and 2 parts vodka for tincture base in a clean glass jar.
Combine the root material with 70 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio by volume and seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to smaller glass bottles with dropper lids and label them for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with cystitis because its potent anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the irritated bladder lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The herb's antimicrobial activity also helps to combat the bacterial infection that often causes cystitis, alleviating symptoms such as burning while urinating and frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, goldenrod's natural astringent properties can help to shrink swollen tissues, promoting healing and relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with Cystitis, providing relief from burning sensations while urinating.
Antimicrobial: Goldenrod's antimicrobial activity helps to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to the development of Cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.
Uric acid dissolving: Goldenrod has been traditionally used to help dissolve uric acid stones and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which is often a symptom of Cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Solidago virgaurea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with cystitis.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like ferulic acid and sinapic acid in Solidago virgaurea tinctures have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections contributing to cystitis.

Saponins: Saponins in Solidago virgaurea, such as polyhydroxylated triterpenes, have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, potentially alleviating cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract.
Leaves: Utilized for their diuretic and antimicrobial properties to aid in flushing out bacteria and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
Flowers: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to help reduce pain and discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers and 75% ethanol to make the tincture.
Combine the dried solidago virgaurea flowers and ethanol in a clean glass container with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solidago virgaurea flowers using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Filter the resulting tincture through a paper filter and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with cystitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract. The tannins present in stinging nettle also have a soothing effect on the bladder lining, alleviating burning sensations and irritation often associated with cystitis. Additionally, stinging nettle's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate symptoms of cystitis, providing natural relief for individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Nettle tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which alleviates symptoms of cystitis like pain, burning, and frequency.

Antimicrobial properties: Nettle tincture's antimicrobial agents inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause cystitis, including E. coli, Staphylococcus saprophyticus, and Klebsiella pneumoniae, reducing the severity and duration of infection.

Uricosuric activity: Nettle tincture contains compounds that increase urine production and reduce uric acid levels in the bloodstream, helping to flush out bacteria, toxins, and excess uric acid from the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, saponins): These compounds help to reduce inflammation and soothe the bladder walls, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Phenolics (specifically, flavonoids): Flavonoids present in Urtica dioica tinctures have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with cystitis.

Triterpenes (specifically, ursolic acid): Ursolic acid, a triterpene found in Urtica dioica, has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the bladder, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in histamine-releasing compounds that help stimulate the body's natural response to fight off the infection causing cystitis.
Roots: The roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections in the urinary tract.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Chop the urtica dioica plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped urtica dioica with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar to separate the plant material from the tincture.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause UTIs. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to soothe and calm the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. This natural remedy can provide relief from frequent urination, burning sensations, and other symptoms of this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with cystitis.
Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial agents can help combat bacterial infections, which are a common cause of cystitis, and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the urinary tract.
Spasmolytic properties: Ginger's compounds can relax smooth muscle tissue in the bladder and urethra, helping to relieve spasms and cramping associated with cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds help alleviate cystitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.

6-Gingerol: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, 6-gingerol helps protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing the severity of cystitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots are also widely used, as they contain similar compounds to rhizomes and are often used interchangeably in herbal medicine.
(Optional) Buds: Ginger buds, being a tender and less fibrous part of the plant, can also be used to make tinctures, but their use is less common compared to rhizomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots in the morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces using a knife or a root cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka or ethanol in a ratio of 1:2 in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with cystitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The herb's diuretic properties also increase urine production, flushing out bacteria and toxins that can exacerbate infection symptoms. Additionally, dandelion tinctures may help to restore the bladder's natural pH balance, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by bacterial overgrowth. By addressing these underlying factors, dandelion tinctures can provide effective relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic effect: Dandelion tincture's ability to increase urine production helps flush out bacteria and toxins that may be contributing to cystitis, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, providing relief from burning sensations and pain associated with cystitis.

Antibacterial activity: The tannins and phenolic acids present in dandelion tincture exhibit antibacterial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to cystitis and preventing the growth of new bacteria.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with cystitis.
Flavonoids (particularly quercetin): Quercetin has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as pain and discomfort.
Saponins: The saponins present in Taraxacum officinale may help soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve cystitis symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and prevent infections in the urinary tract.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help increase urine production and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning.
Chop the harvested plant material into smaller pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine 1 part chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Buchu tinctures helps with cystitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract. The herb's antiseptic qualities also help to combat bacterial infections, which can contribute to the development of cystitis. Additionally, buchu tincture's diuretic effects encourage the body to flush out toxins and excess fluids, promoting a healthier bladder and reducing the frequency and urgency of urination associated with cystitis. By addressing these underlying causes, buchu tinctures provide effective relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Buchu's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Buchu's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain in the bladder and urinary tract, providing relief from symptoms of cystitis such as burning sensation while urinating.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Buchu's tannins and flavonoids have a direct antimicrobial effect on the urinary tract, helping to prevent bacterial growth and reducing the risk of recurrent cystitis infections.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Agathosma betulina have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and the risk of infection.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly gallic acid and ellagic acid, in Agathosma betulina have potent antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which help to combat oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria that can cause cystitis.

Diterpenoids: Diterpenoids, such as 15-oxo-pimaric acid, in Agathosma betulina have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to modulate the immune response, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the bladder and urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Leaves: Used for their diuretic properties to increase urine production and help flush out bacteria that cause cystitis.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and protect the urinary tract from infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh agathosma betulina leaves and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped plant material with 250ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with cystitis because it contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. When taken orally, salicin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the urinary tract, where it reduces inflammation and pain associated with cystitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of white willow tincture help alleviate the discomfort and burning sensation caused by the infection, providing relief from symptoms such as frequency, urgency, and painful urination. This natural remedy can be particularly effective in treating acute cases of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with cystitis, providing relief from discomfort and frequency.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in white willow bark have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: White willow bark also contains antimicrobial compounds that may help to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to cystitis, reducing the severity and duration of the condition.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Helps with cystitis by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and discomfort associated with the condition.
Salicylic acid: Assists in relieving cystitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can help to alleviate the severity of the condition.
Tannins: Contributes to cystitis relief by possessing astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote a healthy balance of the urinary tract's mucous membranes.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used due to their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as pain and frequent urination.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to provide a natural source of salicin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the salix alba bark in early spring when the sap is rising for maximum potency.
Dry the collected bark in a warm place for at least two weeks to prevent mold.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces and measure out 500 grams for the tincture.
Combine the chopped bark with 1000 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks before straining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the urinary tract. The tannins present in yarrow also have astringent effects, which can help to shrink the inflamed tissues and provide relief from discomfort and burning sensations during urination. Additionally, yarrow's natural diuretic properties can help to increase urine flow and flush out bacteria that may be contributing to the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, providing relief from painful cystitis symptoms.
Antimicrobial property: Yarrow's antimicrobial agents, including terpenes and saponins, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause UTIs, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Spasmolytic property: Yarrow's spasmolytic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, relax muscles in the urinary tract, alleviating cramping and spasms associated with cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe the bladder lining to alleviate cystitis symptoms.
Apigenin: As a flavonoid, apigenin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the bladder from oxidative stress and reduce the severity of cystitis.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that may contribute to cystitis, thereby reducing the risk of infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated allowing maximum potency retention.
Chop the achillea millefolium material into small pieces to increase surface area for better solvent penetration.
Combine 1 part chopped achillea millefolium with 2 parts 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn tinctures helps with cystitis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The antimicrobial properties of corn tinctures also help to combat bacterial infections, which are a common cause of cystitis. Additionally, the natural flavonoids present in corn tinctures have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing the severity of symptoms such as burning sensations during urination.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal corn tinctures contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which helps alleviate symptoms such as burning and urgency associated with cystitis.
Antimicrobial properties: The tinctures' antimicrobial activity helps combat bacterial infections that can contribute to cystitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and preventing future occurrences.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Herbal corn tinctures have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections (UTIs) by creating an environment unfavorable to the growth of harmful bacteria in the bladder and urethra.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Zea mays may help soothe the bladder and reduce inflammation associated with cystitis by creating a protective barrier and promoting the expulsion of bacteria.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like ferulic acid in Zea mays may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to cystitis symptoms.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol): Flavonoids in Zea mays may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the bladder lining, thereby alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for cystitis because they contain diosgenin, a saponin compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for cystitis because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the urinary tract.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for cystitis because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat infections in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zea mays in its mature stage with at least 1/4 inch of stem and root intact for tincture quality.
Dry zea mays in a single layer at 25 degrees celsius for 7-10 days to prevent mold growth.
Chop dried zea mays into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part zea mays with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for a 1:2 ratio.
Steep zea mays and vodka mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini tinctures helps with cystitis because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties that target the root causes of the condition. The tincture's extracts, such as cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and saponins, work together to reduce inflammation, soothe the bladder lining, and combat bacterial infections, thereby alleviating symptoms like burning sensation, frequent urination, and pelvic pain. By addressing the underlying issues, zucchini tinctures provide natural relief from cystitis episodes, promoting a healthier urinary tract and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Uva Ursi (Bearberry): Contains arbutin, which has been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the risk of bacterial infections and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Marshmallow Root: Possesses mucilages that can help soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Goldenrod: Has been traditionally used to treat bladder and kidney issues, including cystitis, due to its diuretic properties, which may help increase urine production and flush out bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Zucchini roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including cystitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the zucchini plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cucurbita pepo gourds or purchase dried ones in quantities of one pound per batch.
Chop the fresh gourds into small pieces and combine them with a quart of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for four weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator at 45 degrees fahrenheit for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with cystitis because it contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the bladder and urinary tract. This natural herb has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and reduce symptoms such as burning sensations, frequent urination, and pelvic pain. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the urethra, lemon balm tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from cystitis discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking an alternative to antibiotics or other medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tincture helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as pain and burning.

Uterine relaxant properties: The constituents of lemon balm tincture, particularly citral and geraniol, have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, which can help reduce spasms and cramping associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tincture has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that are commonly found in urinary tract infections, including E. coli, which is a common cause of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate urinary tract discomfort associated with cystitis.
Leaves: Abundant in flavonoids and terpenes, the leaves have natural antibacterial and antispasmodic properties that help soothe bladder inflammation and reduce symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at peak potency typically between june and october.
Chop the leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and drying.
Mix one part of chopped melissa leaves with 2 parts of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for slow percolation and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle and label with contents dosage and preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with cystitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the urinary tract, reducing pain and discomfort associated with bladder infections. The herb's antispasmodic effects also help to relax the muscles in the bladder, allowing for easier urination and reduced frequency of trips to the bathroom. Additionally, valerian tinctures have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection causing cystitis, promoting faster healing and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian root's sedative and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain, discomfort, and inflammation associated with cystitis, allowing for more effective treatment and recovery.

Antimicrobial properties: Valerian root has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that cause cystitis, such as Escherichia coli (E. coli), by inhibiting their growth and preventing the spread of infection.

Uroprotective effects: Valerian root's uroprotective effects can help soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing the risk of further irritation and damage caused by cystitis, and promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, the roots have a calming effect on the urinary tract, making them effective in treating the inflammation and pain associated with cystitis.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of valerian have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the bladder lining and reduce swelling, providing relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 500 grams of fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces using scissors.
Combine chopped roots with 1000 milliliters of pure grain alcohol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep mixture for six weeks shaking jar twice daily to ensure proper extraction and infusion.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape tinctures helps with cystitis because they contain a combination of natural compounds that work together to provide relief from urinary tract discomfort. The antioxidant-rich properties of grapes help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated bladder lining, while the antibacterial properties combat any underlying infections. Additionally, grape tincture's ability to relax muscles in the pelvic area helps to ease cramps and spasms associated with cystitis, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Grape tincture contains antibacterial compounds like resveratrol, which helps to inhibit the growth of bacteria like E. coli that can cause cystitis, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Grape tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from painful symptoms like burning while urinating.

Antioxidant properties: Grape tincture is rich in antioxidants that help protect the bladder lining and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation, which can contribute to cystitis development and recurrence.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the roots are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of terpenoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal activities, helping to prevent infections that can exacerbate cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitis vinifera leaves in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 200g of fresh vitis vinifera leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any remaining plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tincture's flavonoids and anthocyanins have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis. Additionally, elder's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause the condition, promoting a healthy balance of bacteria in the bladder and urethra. By addressing both inflammation and infection, elder tinctures offer natural support for managing cystitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Antibacterial properties: Elder tincture's antibacterial compounds help combat bacteria such as E. coli that can cause UTIs, including cystitis, by inhibiting their growth and multiplication.

Urinary antiseptic and anti-adhesive properties: Elder tincture helps prevent bacteria from adhering to the bladder wall and urinary tract, making it more difficult for them to cause infection and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and combating bacterial infections associated with cystitis.
Barks: Elder barks contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can help alleviate cystitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at peak blooming and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried sambucus nigra flowers into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and combine them with vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the herbal mixture in vodka at a ratio of one part sambucus nigra to two parts vodka for a minimum of 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool dark place.
Label and store the finished sambucus nigra tincture at a dosage of 20-30 drops, three times daily, for 4 weeks to help alleviate cystitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with cystitis because of its mucilaginous properties, which soothe and protect the bladder lining. This natural demulcent coating reduces inflammation and irritation caused by bacterial infections, such as E. coli, while also providing a barrier against further infection. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to calm the burning sensation associated with cystitis, offering fast and effective relief from uncomfortable symptoms like urinary frequency and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract, providing relief from painful urination and other symptoms of cystitis.

Soothing mucilage: The mucilage extracted from slippery elm bark forms a protective coating on the mucous membranes in the bladder and urethra, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissues and reduce inflammation.
Antimicrobial properties: Slippery elm's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to cystitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are valued for their ability to soothe and calm irritated bladder tissues, making them an effective remedy for alleviating symptoms of cystitis.
Barks: Slippery elm bark is rich in mucilage, a protective substance that helps to shield the urinary tract from inflammation and irritation, reducing discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ulmus rubra leaves and stems in quantities of 250 grams for a single batch.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces and combine it with 500 milliliters of vodka or everclear at 20% w/w ratio.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar with a lid and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, label them, and store them in the refrigerator at 3-5 degrees celsius for up to one year with a dosage of 15-30 drops per serving.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cystitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are urinary tract infections, interstitial cystitis, and overactive bladder. Herbal remedies like Staphysagria, Berberis, and Uva ursi help to soothe the bladder and reduce inflammation, while others like Juniperus and Apium may help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with cystitis is a blend of Uva Ursi, Juniper Berry, and Marshmallow Root. Uva Ursi helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, while Juniper Berry aids in relieving pain and promoting urination. Marshmallow Root soothes the bladder and urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort. This combination can be taken in a diluted form, typically 20-30 drops per glass of water, and should be repeated as needed to alleviate cystitis symptoms. Consult a healthcare professional for proper usage.
